title: chamaesyce celastroides var. kohalana
intro: Chamaesyce celastroides var. kohalana, also known as Kohala Spurge, is a variety of flowering plant in the Euphorbiaceae family, native to Hawaii. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid and terpenoid compounds. The health benefits of Kohala Spurge include wound healing, reducing inflammation, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In Hawaiian traditional medicine, Kohala Spurge is prepared as a tea, poultice, or salve to utilize its medicinal properties.
